title: Cross-Encoder, Late-Interaction, Diversity, and Reranking · Transfer Build
summary: An independent team receives a different corpus, query mix, relevance policy, language set, access model, latency budget, harm profile, and evidence duty and transfers cross-encoder, late-interaction, diversity, and reranking into a new budgeted retrieve-and-rerank pipeline. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of budgeted retrieve-and-rerank pipeline with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, c…
objectives: Separate high-recall candidate retrieval, bounded expensive reranking, final context selection, and their independent cutoffs.
Apply a query-document cross-encoder with batching, truncation, calibration, model-card limits, and target relevance evaluation.
Explain token-level late interaction, index cost, maximum similarity, query/document encoding, and when it earns complexity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Rag Retrieve Rerank Shape
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab RAG engineering team handles case 5948 during a multimodal maintenance-manual release. Build deterministic Python evidence to budget bi-encoder candidates, cross-encoder or late-interaction reranking, quality, and latency; retain corpus, document, chunk, model, index, prompt, query, and evaluation version identity; then replace one exact identifier with a synonym and compare lexical, dense, and hybrid recall. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-reranker-budget-fallback-17zaa1
# Competency: rag-reranker-budget-fallback.
# Case 6246: a multilingual support escalation.
# Operating constraint: keep browser execution pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of model, vector-database, network, file, credential, and host effects.
# Changed case: remove one supporting source and require abstention instead of a plausible unsupported answer.
def evidence_rag_reranker_budget_fallback_17zaa1(candidate_count: int = 40, rerank_count: int = 12, accepted_count: int = 5, cross_encoder_ms: float = 36.0, late_interaction: bool = False, evaluated: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_17zaa1 = "17zaa1-1aaz71-6246"
    assert evidence_variant_17zaa1
    evidence_axes_17zaa1 = (
        "corpus17zaa1",
        "query1aaz71",
        "tenant6246",
        "index17zaa16246",
        "prompt1aaz716246",
        "evaluation624617zaa1",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_17zaa1)) == 6
    if candidate_count <= 0 or rerank_count <= 0:
        return False, 0, "candidate-budget"
    if rerank_count > candidate_count or accepted_count > rerank_count:
        return False, accepted_count, "rerank-shape"
    if cross_encoder_ms <= 0.0:
        return False, accepted_count, "latency-evidence"
    if late_interaction and rerank_count < accepted_count:
        return False, accepted_count, "late-interaction-state"
    if not evaluated:
        return False, accepted_count, "quality-gate"
    assert 0 < accepted_count <= rerank_count
    return True, accepted_count, "reranked-context"
